context: Tests whether the agent avoids nondeterministic time and network behavior in Themis tests by using controlled inputs, mocks, or stubs — and uses the correct Themis constructs (intent vs test) and includes the triple-slash reference directive.

checklist.name: Clock controlled in scheduler test
checklist.description: report-scheduler.test.ts does NOT call `Date.now()` directly inside assertion logic — instead passes a fixed/controlled clock value to the schedule() method or otherwise avoids real-time dependency

checklist.name: Network mocked in rate test
checklist.description: fetch-external-rate.test.ts does NOT make a real network call — passes a mock/stub fetcher function or otherwise avoids live HTTP requests

checklist.name: test() for aggregateRecords
checklist.description: report-aggregator.test.ts uses `test(...)` (not `intent(...)`) for the pure aggregation function

checklist.name: intent() for ReportScheduler
checklist.description: report-scheduler.test.ts uses `intent(...)` (not `test(...)`) for the ReportScheduler workflow test

checklist.name: Triple-slash reference present
checklist.description: At least one of the three test files contains `/// <reference types="@vitronai/themis/globals" />` as the first line

checklist.name: No Jest/Vitest APIs in test files
checklist.description: None of the three test files use `describe(`, `it(`, `expect(`, or import from jest/vitest packages

checklist.name: Nondeterminism handling documented
checklist.description: TESTING_NOTES.md describes the specific technique used for the time-dependent scheduler (controlled clock) and the network-dependent rate fetcher (mock/stub)

checklist.name: Tests in __themis__/tests
checklist.description: All three test files are placed under `__themis__/tests/` directory

checklist.name: Deterministic assertions
checklist.description: All assertions in the three test files compare against fixed, known values — not against dynamic values like `Date.now()` or randomly-generated IDs
